_note: Reviewer follow-up #5: `<svelte:component this={X} let:item>` and `<svelte:self let:item>` should consume the synthetic component's default slot — same shape as a regular `<Comp let:item>`. Pre-fix the special-element path hardcoded `false` for let-handling so any `let:foo` directive was silently dropped: `foo` was undeclared inside the children body and TS2304 fired on every reference (`Cannot find name 'item'`). Post-fix the destructure lands as `const { $$_$$, item, … } = inst.$$slot_def.default; $$_$$;` so `item` is in scope. `inst.$$slot_def` resolves to `any` for the synthetic component root (full SlotHandler port for typed slot defs is deferred), so `item: any` doesn't constrain consumer access — but the declaration must exist for the children walk to type-check. Mirrors upstream `InlineComponent.ts:166`.
